Promise: 52 city-owned vacant lots reserved for new housing with units affordable to residents making $17,500 per year.
Outcome: The city sold two lots. One housing development is completed.

Program: Preservation of Existing Affordable Rental (PEAR) program
Promise: $1.5M in grants to buy or refinance multifamily properties in exchange for 30-year affordability agreements.
Outcome: No applications were received. The $1.5M in funding was "reallocated," according to DOH.

Promise: $500,000 granted to nonproftit developer POAH to acquire and redevelop residential units in Woodlawn.
Outcome: To date, POAH has not used the money.

Promise: $7 million dollars loan fund for local developrs to bring vacant lots and apartment buidlings back online.
Outcome: 40 units were financed. No single family home projects got off the ground.

Program: Long-term Homeowner Home Improvement Grant Program
Promise: $1 million dollars granted to longterm homeowners in Woodlawn who need help fixing up their properties.
City required developers on city-owned land to submit plans for local hiring, outreach, and training.
Outcome: One developer submitted a plan for 19 jobs and hired three people.

Program: Tenant Right of First Refusal pilot program
Promise: Give Woodlawn renters the first chance to match or beat outside offers on their buildings before a sale goes through.
Outcome: To date, no tenants have exercised their right of first refusal.
